rows.boundary: This establishes a manuscript framing and comparison map, not a claim that every cited system was fully reproduced.
rows.evidence: Related-work and contribution matrices position the method against BDD, LUT/ROS, XAG/MC, ABC/RevKit/mockturtle/CirKit, SSHR, and learning-guided synthesis.
rows.next_action: Update this row only if the manuscript's claimed novelty or related-work scope changes.

rows.boundary: No hardware placement, routing, native-gate scheduling, noise model, or magic-state-factory resource estimate is claimed.
rows.evidence: The abstract, limitations, and availability text state that results are logical-layer estimates and exclude hardware mapping/routing/noise claims.
rows.next_action: Keep this boundary unless new hardware-mapping experiments are added.

rows.boundary: Correctness is tied to ANF, emitted-circuit, truth-table, or phase checks; learned components only rank, gate, or allocate search.
rows.evidence: The method workflow covers input normalization, candidate generation, neural/MCTS/beam control, guarded selection, circuit emission, and verification/reporting.
rows.next_action: Keep workflow artifacts aligned with any new candidate generator or verification stage.

rows.boundary: The evidence supports bounded neural/search-control contributions, not a claim that deep RL alone explains all gains.
rows.evidence: Learned-control audit separates promoted frontier/gating/phase-pruning controls from limited diagnostics and reports held-out quality/time tradeoffs.
rows.next_action: Do not promote runtime-negative or quality-weak neural diagnostics as main contributions.

rows.boundary: External rows are logic-level probes or exact-oracle reversible probes; they are not full ROS or hardware-mapped reproductions.
rows.evidence: Comparison evidence covers internal logical baselines, SSHR, ABC/BDD, ROS-style LUT, mockturtle, CirKit, RevKit CLI exact-oracle, and phase/Rz probes.
rows.next_action: Use the comparability audit when writing any baseline claim.

rows.boundary: The strongest claim is T-count and weighted-score improvement; raw CNOT/depth/ancilla tradeoffs remain explicit.
rows.evidence: Paired statistics show score/T improvements over direct ANF, ESOP, SSHR variants, ABC-XAG, ROS-style LUT, mockturtle, CirKit, RevKit CLI, and high-dimensional root/fast baselines.
rows.next_action: Keep effect-size claims paired and matched by function name.

rows.boundary: Many strong baselines remain incomparable on raw resources; this is a controlled limitation rather than hidden negative evidence.
rows.evidence: Raw Pareto dominance is computed separately on T-count, CNOT, depth, and peak ancilla so weighted-score wins are not overstated.
rows.next_action: Do not convert weighted-score wins into universal raw-resource dominance claims.

rows.boundary: High-dimensional rows are symbolic or generated bridge checks, not exhaustive truth-table enumeration for all n=26--40 functions.
rows.evidence: Scaling audit covers n=20,24,28,32,40 term-set/symbolic slices and complete truth-table bridge checks for n=21--25 generated functions.
rows.next_action: Keep large-scale claims tied to the scaling audit and bridge scope.

rows.boundary: Wall-clock timings are workstation context; portable claims remain logical resources and verification status.
rows.evidence: Compute audit records Apple M4 Pro CPU/GPU/MPS availability, worker counts up to 10, and external tool commit provenance.
rows.next_action: Rerun reproducibility audit after adding scripts, raw data, summaries, manifests, tables, or figures.

rows.boundary: Counts describe manuscript artifacts, not independent experimental datasets.
rows.evidence: Current package has 7 submitted figure panels and 151 generated paper tables.
rows.next_action: Spot-check figure/table readability after every layout or data update.

rows.boundary: Author names, target-venue formatting choices, and final declarations remain external author inputs.
rows.evidence: Compiled submission PDF exists with 26 pages and the TeX source is present.
rows.next_action: After author metadata is supplied, recompile and rerun readiness checks.

rows.boundary: The lightweight rebuild does not rerun raw sweeps, external probes, or neural training jobs.
rows.evidence: Rebuild script regenerates paper-facing outputs and the payload archive; archive and SHA256 sidecar are present.
rows.next_action: Rerun the rebuild script after any payload-affecting edit.

rows.boundary: These fields cannot be inferred from code or experiments and must be confirmed by the author or target venue.
rows.evidence: Submission metadata audit reports 11 author/venue field group(s) needing author input.
rows.next_action: Fill author order, affiliations, ORCIDs, funding, acknowledgements, competing interests, archive links, license, disclosure, and target-venue fields.

rows.boundary: The research/package side is audit-complete, but the full objective is not closed until author and target-venue metadata are supplied.
rows.evidence: Metadata audit reports 11 human-gated group(s); readiness audit remains the paper/package gate and is cited as a separate evidence file.
rows.next_action: Do not mark the objective complete until the author-specific declarations and final archive/venue links are filled and audits rerun.
